ALL SPECIALTIES / DIETITIAN / HAWAII

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Hawaii Dietitian License

Hawaii Dietitian license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-02-25 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Dietitian applications in Hawaii run through State Licensing Section - Dietitians. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Paper Application - Obtained by calling the DOH and completed accurately.
  • CDR Registration Card - A copy of your active Commission on Dietetic Registration card.
  • Official College Transcripts - Proof of the required accredited degree (foreign degrees require validation).

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 60 Calendar Days
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Hawaii allows for licensure by endorsement. Individuals holding a valid dietitian certification or license from another state, territory, or jurisdiction may be eligible if the requirements are equal to or greater than Hawaii's (maintaining an active CDR typically qualifies).

  1. STEP 1

    No State Licensure Required

    Hawaii state law does not mandate licensure for individuals practicing as dietitians or nutritionists. There is no state application process through the Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs (DCCA) or any other state entity.

    Fee: $225 · Timeline: 60 Calendar Days

  2. STEP 2

    National Certification is Voluntary

    While not a state requirement, many professionals pursue national certification, such as the Registered Dietitian Nutritionist (RDN) credential from the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR), for professional recognition and marketability. This is a national, not state, process.

    Fee: $225 · Timeline: 60 Calendar Days

  3. STEP 3

    Employer and Payer Requirements

    Prospective employers or insurance providers in Hawaii may still require national certifications (e.g., RDN) or specific academic qualifications to hire dietitians or for reimbursement purposes, even without state licensure.

    Fee: $225 · Timeline: 60 Calendar Days

  4. STEP 4

    Review Professional Standards

    Even without state licensure, professionals are encouraged to adhere to ethical guidelines and standards of practice established by national professional organizations like the Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics.

    Fee: $225 · Timeline: 60 Calendar Days

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$225
Background check
$0 (included in application)
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$225

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
State Licensing Section - Dietitians
Phone
(808) 586-3000
Website
health.hawaii.gov/ohca/
Application page
Open application

Renewal

Dietitian renewal in Hawaii follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 3 years (Must maintain active CDR)
Renewal fee
Varies (Contact DOH)
Continuing education
75 hours every 5 years, must maintain active CDR

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does a Hawaii dietitian license cost?

The initial non-refundable fee for a Hawaii Dietitian License is $225.

When does the Hawaii license need to be renewed?

Hawaii dietitian licenses are valid for three years from the date of issue and must be renewed triennially. You should contact the DOH to confirm current renewal fees.

Can I apply for a Hawaii dietitian license online?

No. You must contact the State Licensing Section directly at (808) 692-7400 to request the application packet.

Does Hawaii have reciprocity for dietitians?

No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Hawaii. To work in Hawaii, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Hawaii?

Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

Can I start working in Hawaii while my endorsement application is processing?

Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$225

Processing time

60 Calendar Days

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Not available

National exam

Registration Examination for Dietitians

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Every 3 years (Must maintain active CDR) · Varies (Contact DOH)

Licensing board

State Licensing Section - Dietitians

health.hawaii.gov/ohca/

(808) 586-3000
